items.title: Reference MCP: Search your Codex chats from Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer built an MCP server specifically to solve the headache of getting Claude Code to reference decisions made in Codex chats. Since you use both tools to build AI-ingest, this bridges the gap and keeps your context unified without manual copy-pasting. This week, install this MCP server to let your Claude Code terminal search and reference your previous Codex design chats.

items.title: Argus: Run repetitive Claude Code workflows with 80% fewer tokens
items.why_it_matters: This tool optimizes repetitive AI workflows specifically for Claude Code, claiming to slash token usage by up to 80%. Since you want to keep your development costs down while letting the AI do the heavy lifting, this is a direct way to optimize your API spend. This week, install the Argus CLI and run your next repetitive code-generation task through it to see how many tokens you save.

items.title: How a 'retry storm' can drain your LLM API wallet overnight
items.why_it_matters: This post-mortem details how a simple loop error caused an app to repeatedly call an LLM API, resulting in a massive, unexpected bill. As you build and deploy AI-ingest, protecting your API keys from infinite loops is critical to keeping your project viable. This week, check your AI-ingest backend code and add a basic limit or exponential backoff to your API call functions so a failed request never loops infinitely.

items.title: SpecManager: A product manager plugin for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This tool acts as a Claude Code plugin that helps you manage feature specifications and checklists directly in your terminal. It is designed to help you focus on the 'direction' part of vibe coding by turning your ideas into structured, verifiable tasks before you write code. This week, install SpecManager and use it to generate a step-by-step implementation checklist for the next feature you want to add to AI-ingest.

items.title: Stop copy-pasting terminal errors into Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This workflow guide explains why manually copy-pasting terminal errors into Claude Code wastes tokens and degrades the AI's context. Letting Claude Code run the commands or read the logs natively provides cleaner context, leading to faster, more accurate fixes. This week, the next time your Next.js build fails, run the build command directly inside Claude Code instead of copy-pasting the error from your external terminal.
